diagrams.title: Binary Tree Terminology
diagrams.description: Shows a labeled binary tree with root, internal nodes, leaf nodes, left/right children, parent, depth levels, and height marked
elements: Level 2: D (left of B), E (right of B), F (left of C)
Leaf nodes: D, E, F, G
Parent of D is B, Parent of C is A
Height of tree = 3 (from A to G)
Depth of G = 3 edges from root

diagrams.title: Tree Types Comparison
diagrams.description: Three side-by-side trees showing full, complete, and perfect binary trees with 7 nodes
elements: Perfect: All levels full - pyramid shape with 1,2,4 nodes
Complete: All filled except last level, left-aligned
Full: Each node has 0 or 2 children, no single children

diagrams.title: Traversal Visualization
diagrams.description: Same binary tree with nodes labeled, showing order of visitation for all four traversals
elements: Tree: Root A, B left, C right, D left of B, E right of B, F left of C, G right of C
